code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    single, readable sentence.  The function uses a small set of handcrafted
    templates for the supported predicates and joins the resulting clauses
    with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Basic templates for the most common astronaut predicates.
    # Each template receives (subject, object) and returns a clause string.
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    templates = {
        # Astronaut personal data
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {_clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {_clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {_clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {_clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {_clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {_clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {_clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a citizen of {_clean(o)}",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {_clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{_clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {_clean(o)} ribbon",
        # Mission‑related predicates
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was on the {o} mission",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was operated by {_clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {s}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was the commander of {s}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a crew member of {s}",
        # Miscellaneous
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{s} was represented by {_clean(o)}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was also known as {_clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} earned {_clean(o)} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the award {_clean(o)}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {_clean(o)}",
        "fossil": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is a fossil from {s}",
        # Generic fallback
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{s} {_clean(o)}",
    }

    # Build clauses directly from templates; each clause already contains the subject where appropriate.
    clauses = [templates.get(t.predicate, templates["default"])(t.subject, t.object) for t in triples]

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
